body.title: Make silent ollama→cloud fallback noisier (budget-surprise prevention)
body.result: High-urgency cc_inbox push on _call_tier2 ollama→OR fallback. Companion to routing log line from T-cert-debugger-env-mirror. 2 regression tests. 221 related tests pass.
body.description:
Today pe_chain fell back from local Ollama to paid OR cloud without any visible alert — Akien noticed only because it showed up in the forensic log. For a cloud call that costs budget, the routing decision should be surfaced (cc_alerts, milieu arousal bump, or dashboard banner) so it's not a silent surprise.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (fallback branch in _call_tier2 when Ollama unreachable + OR key present). Possibly cc_alerts.log append + a milieu urgency bump.
**Design rules:** docs-in-code.
**Scope boundary:** IN — one-line alert when the fallback engages (cc_alerts entry + optional stderr log at WARNING). OUT — gating the fallback behind an env flag (that's a separate policy question); building a full budget-guard wrapper (also separate — part of T-budget-gate-before-cloud if it exists).
**Test plan:** unit test: _call_tier2 with Ollama unreachable + OR key present writes a cc_alerts entry before dispatching cloud call.
**Gated on:** T-qwen-only-pe-chain-cloud (smaller blast radius and tighter scope sequencing).
